title: Runtime Validation, Parsing, and Trust Boundaries · Contract Defense
summary: Parse API, storage, URL, form, and message values into trustworthy domain records without unsafe casts. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a runtime-validated public data adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Receive external JSON, storage, DOM, environment, and message data as unknown until evidence narrows it.
Build parsers that return typed values or structured failure paths rather than booleans that discard evidence.
Handle required, optional, deprecated, defaulted, and versioned fields with changed-schema fixtures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the External Unknown evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps External Unknown assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: An unknown payload is alternately {kind:"ok", value:3}, {kind:"error", message:"timeout"}, and {kind:"ok", value:"3"}. Validate runtime shape before narrowing, retain the rejection reason, and make the final branch exhaustive.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so External Unknown has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the External Unknown precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: An unknown payload is alternately {kind:"ok", value:3}, {kind:"error", message:"timeout"}, and {kind:"ok", value:"3"}. Validate runtime shape before narrowing, retain the rejection reason, and make the final branch exhaustive.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A generic JSON request helper can know the server response type without validation.

options.text: Declare the domain relationship and identify every untrusted runtime boundary
Infer or annotate the smallest sound type and validate unknown values before narrowing
Run strict diagnostics, inspect emitted JavaScript, and execute the changed case
Add compile-time rejection and runtime boundary evidence, then document remaining limits

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Unsafe Assertion Audit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An unknown payload is alternately {kind:"ok", value:5}, {kind:"error", message:"timeout"}, and {kind:"ok", value:"5"}. Validate runtime shape before narrowing, retain the rejection reason, and make the final branch exhaustive.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Locate and replace as, non-null, double assertions, any, and suppression directives with evidence-producing boundaries.
